steps.label: cause
steps.text: Fermentation begins with yeast populations naturally present on grapes and in the winery rather than one selected commercial strain.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: A succession of strains can produce a broader and less predictable range of metabolites before robust Saccharomyces populations complete fermentation.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine may show distinctive local or complex character, but fermentation speed, completion and aroma profile are less predictable.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: diverse local yeast populations may increase distinctive expression while reducing process predictability.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
